steps.title: Locate the key contrast in the passage
steps.content:
The text distinguishes between Mieszko II Lambert — documented as king from 1025 to 1031 CE — and Siemomysł, whose "historical actuality is disputed" and whose chronicle record relies on oral tradition "unlike its records of later rulers." The question asks for a difference in how historians view each figure.
  • Choice A — 'Historians agree that Mieszko II Lambert existed, but disagree about whether Siemomysł existed.': Directly reflects the text: Mieszko II Lambert has firm documented dates; Siemomysł's existence is disputed. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Historians believe that the Gesta principum Polonorum provides more evidence for Siemomysł's existence than it does for Mieszko II Lambert's existence.': The opposite is true — the chronicle's record of Siemomysł relies on oral tradition, making it less reliable than its records of later rulers. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Historians agree that Siemomysł ruled Poland much later than Mieszko II Lambert.': The text says Siemomysł supposedly ruled in the 10th century (before 1025), so he ruled earlier, not later. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Historians find the orally transmitted stories affirming the existence of Mieszko II Lambert to be more convincing than similar stories about Siemomysł.': The text does not mention oral tradition concerning Mieszko II Lambert at all; the comparison cannot be made. ✗

steps.title: Verify the supported claim
steps.content: The text explicitly says Siemomysł "is said to have ruled" and his actuality "is disputed," while Mieszko II Lambert "ruled as king from 1025 to 1031 CE" with no such qualification. The oral-tradition note further weakens confidence in Siemomysł, confirming historians do not all accept his existence. That is the correct answer.
